data: show me all open bugs
Listed open bug issues from Redmine
show me all open bugs
direct

There are 20 open bug issues: 6 in the Mobile App project, 13 in the E-Learning Platform, and 1 in the web Platform. Four of these bugs are overdue. Notable urgent issues include #9 (XSS vulnerability in the course discussion forum) and #8 (course completion certificate shows the wrong student name). Several payment‑crash bugs in the Mobile App remain unassigned.

Would you like me to take any action on this?

what are the risks this week?
Identified risks and overdue issues for the week
what are the risks this week?
risk_agent

Risk 1: Overdue critical issues in E-Learning Platform — 5 issues are 3–45 days late (#21, #22, #28, #24, #7), risking release delays and user trust. → Immediately assign a clear owner to each (already assigned) and start a 15‑minute daily sync to track progress.

Risk 2: High number of unassigned issues in Mobile App — 6 open issues have no owner, risking missed sprint goals and work falling through cracks. → Conduct a triage meeting today to assign each unassigned issue based on tracker and priority (e.g., bugs to Abir/Amira, features to Alice/Amir).

Risk 3: Workload concentration on Alice and Abir — each carries 8 and 6 open issues (including overdue), risking burnout and further delays. → Move 2 low‑priority tasks from each to Amir or Amira (who have 4 and 5 open issues respectively) to rebalance load this week.

any overdue issues?
Found and listed overdue issues across projects
any overdue issues?
risk_agent

- Six issues are currently overdue, impacting on‑time delivery.  
- The most delayed item is #21 (Add real‑time chat) – 45 days overdue, assigned to Amir (Backend).  
- Next are #22 (Video streaming freezes) – 30 days overdue (Abir, Mobile) and #28/#24 (Course search & homepage lazy‑load) – each 4 days overdue (Alice & Amira).  
- Remaining overdue items: #7 (Mobile app crash) – 3 days overdue (Abir) and #37 (Checkout error) – 2 days overdue (Alice).  
- Prioritize resolving #21 and #22 first, then address the 4‑day items to mitigate further schedule risk.
